descriptionText: M/Y Savannah For Sale 2016 |Guest Cabins:6| CrewCabins:12The opportunity to consider M/Y Savannah For Sale 2016 will interest anyone looking for a yacht that marked a significant moment in modern yacht design. Delivered by Feadship in 2016, Savannah quickly became known as the world’s first hybrid superyacht, combining advanced engineering with a distinctive exterior profile. Today, she remains one of the most recognisable vessels in her size category, and she presents in good condition thanks to consistent care and professional management since launch.Yacht Main Specifications: • Brand: Feadship • Year: 2016 • Length: 83 m • Max. Beam: 12.2 m • Construction: Aluminium • Draught: 3.85 m • Gross Tonnage: 2,250 GT • Fuel Tank: 170,000 l • Water Tank: 40,000 l • Guest Cabins: 6 • Crew Cabins: 12 • Engines: 1 x Wärtsilä 1800 kW @ 1000 rpmFeadship built M/Y Savannah For Sale 2016 to showcase a new approach to efficiency and contemporary yacht living. At 83 metres in length, she was a major step forward in blending eco‑conscious propulsion with large‑yacht comfort. Her hybrid system, built around a Wärtsilä 1800 kW engine, delivers flexible operation that can shift between diesel, electric, or a combination of the two. When running in battery mode, Savannah is notably quiet, making slow cruising particularly pleasant for those on board. These design choices allowed her to achieve meaningful reductions in fuel consumption compared to similar yachts from her era.Her profile is instantly identifiable thanks to the metallic green hull and the ‘floating’ superstructure effect, both of which became design talking points at the time of launch. Feadship also prioritised openness and natural light inside, most notably with the aft owner and main deck areas that create a large indoor‑outdoor space. Features such as the semi‑submerged Nemo lounge added a new way of observing the surroundings at water level, demonstrating how design could enhance the experience of being at sea.Savannah’s impact was widely recognised. In 2016 she received the World Superyacht Award for Motoryacht of the Year and also won in the category for Best Displacement Motor Yacht between 1,300 GT and 2,999 GT. Additional acknowledgements came from the ShowBoats Design Awards, where she was recognised for her interior, exterior, and a holistic design approach. The ISS Awards further highlighted her influence by naming her Best Power Yacht over 65m. These awards reflected not only her technological innovation but also the practical and thoughtful way her spaces were arranged and executed.With accommodation for guests across six cabins and generous quarters for a professional crew, Savannah was designed for extended cruising. Her 2,250 GT volume provides a sense of comfort and space that aligns with larger yachts, while her 12.2‑metre beam ensures broad deck areas and well‑balanced interior layouts. The yacht has been well maintained through the years and remains in good condition, keeping her ready for a new owner who appreciates her place in modern yacht history.Anyone reviewing M/Y Savannah For Sale 2016 will find a vessel that introduced meaningful innovation, combined with the craftsmanship expected from Feadship.
descriptionHtml:
M/Y Savannah For Sale 2016 |Guest Cabins:6| CrewCabins:12

The opportunity to consider M/Y Savannah For Sale 2016 will interest anyone looking for a yacht that marked a significant moment in modern yacht design. Delivered by Feadship in 2016, Savannah quickly became known as the world’s first hybrid superyacht, combining advanced engineering with a distinctive exterior profile. Today, she remains one of the most recognisable vessels in her size category, and she presents in good condition thanks to consistent care and professional management since launch.

Yacht Main Specifications:
• Brand: Feadship
• Year: 2016
• Length: 83 m
• Max. Beam: 12.2 m
• Construction: Aluminium
• Draught: 3.85 m
• Gross Tonnage: 2,250 GT
• Fuel Tank: 170,000 l
• Water Tank: 40,000 l
• Guest Cabins: 6
• Crew Cabins: 12
• Engines: 1 x Wärtsilä 1800 kW @ 1000 rpm

Feadship built M/Y Savannah For Sale 2016 to showcase a new approach to efficiency and contemporary yacht living. At 83 metres in length, she was a major step forward in blending eco‑conscious propulsion with large‑yacht comfort. Her hybrid system, built around a Wärtsilä 1800 kW engine, delivers flexible operation that can shift between diesel, electric, or a combination of the two. When running in battery mode, Savannah is notably quiet, making slow cruising particularly pleasant for those on board. These design choices allowed her to achieve meaningful reductions in fuel consumption compared to similar yachts from her era.

Her profile is instantly identifiable thanks to the metallic green hull and the ‘floating’ superstructure effect, both of which became design talking points at the time of launch. Feadship also prioritised openness and natural light inside, most notably with the aft owner and main deck areas that create a large indoor‑outdoor space. Features such as the semi‑submerged Nemo lounge added a new way of observing the surroundings at water level, demonstrating how design could enhance the experience of being at sea.

Savannah’s impact was widely recognised. In 2016 she received the World Superyacht Award for Motoryacht of the Year and also won in the category for Best Displacement Motor Yacht between 1,300 GT and 2,999 GT. Additional acknowledgements came from the ShowBoats Design Awards, where she was recognised for her interior, exterior, and a holistic design approach. The ISS Awards further highlighted her influence by naming her Best Power Yacht over 65m. These awards reflected not only her technological innovation but also the practical and thoughtful way her spaces were arranged and executed.

With accommodation for guests across six cabins and generous quarters for a professional crew, Savannah was designed for extended cruising. Her 2,250 GT volume provides a sense of comfort and space that aligns with larger yachts, while her 12.2‑metre beam ensures broad deck areas and well‑balanced interior layouts. The yacht has been well maintained through the years and remains in good condition, keeping her ready for a new owner who appreciates her place in modern yacht history.

Anyone reviewing M/Y Savannah For Sale 2016 will find a vessel that introduced meaningful innovation, combined with the craftsmanship expected from Feadship.
